subject: ap-precalculus
title: Logarithmic Regression: Soil Temperature & Depth
stimulus:
A geologist measures soil temperature (in degrees Celsius) at various depths below the surface and records the following data:

| Depth $d$ (meters) | Temperature $T$ ($^\circ$C) |
|---|---|
| 1 | 12.0 |
| 4 | 18.4 |
| 9 | 22.6 |
| 16 | 26.0 |
| 25 | 28.7 |

The geologist proposes the model $T(d) = a + b\ln(d)$ to represent soil temperature as a function of depth.

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the data at $d = 1$ and $d = 25$ to write two equations that can be used to find the values of constants $a$ and $b$.

(ii) Find the values of $a$ and $b$ as decimal approximations. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: The first equation immediately gives $a = 12$ since $\ln(1) = 0$. The second equation then gives $b = 16.7 / \ln(25) \approx 5.189$. Decimal must be correct to three decimal places. Full credit for both values if shown correctly. Accept $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ as a complete response.

scoring_points.description: Two correct equations — $a + b\ln(1) = 12.0$, which simplifies to $a = 12.0$; and $a + b\ln(25) = 28.7$; equivalently, $12 + b\ln(25) = 28.7$

required_elements: Two correct equations — $a + b\ln(1) = 12.0$, which simplifies to $a = 12.0$; and $a + b\ln(25) = 28.7$; equivalently, $12 + b\ln(25) = 28.7$
alternatives.correct_example: Two correct equations — $a + b\ln(1) = 12.0$, which simplifies to $a = 12.0$; and $a + b\ln(25) = 28.7$; equivalently, $12 + b\ln(25) = 28.7$

scoring_points.description: Correct values $a = 12.0$ and $b = \frac{28.7 - 12.0}{\ln(25)} = \frac{16.7}{3.2189} \approx 5.189$ (accept values in $[5.18, 5.20]$); supporting work must show the substitution and division step

required_elements: Correct values $a = 12.0$ and $b = \frac{28.7 - 12.0}{\ln(25)} = \frac{16.7}{3.2189} \approx 5.189$ (accept values in $[5.18, 5.20]$); supporting work must show the substitution and division step
alternatives.correct_example: Correct values $a = 12.0$ and $b = \frac{28.7 - 12.0}{\ln(25)} = \frac{16.7}{3.2189} \approx 5.189$ (accept values in $[5.18, 5.20]$); supporting work must show the substitution and division step

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the model $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ to predict the soil temperature at a depth of $d = 50$ meters. Express your answer as a decimal approximation.

(ii) Find the depth $d$ at which the model predicts a soil temperature of $30^\circ$C. Express your answer as a decimal approximation. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), $\ln(50) \approx 3.9120$, so $5.189 \times 3.9120 \approx 20.30$, giving $T \approx 32.30$°C. For part (ii), solving $5.189\ln(d) = 18$ gives $\ln(d) = 18/5.189 \approx 3.469$, so $d = e^{3.469} \approx 32.1$ m. Supporting work must show both the isolation of $\ln(d)$ and the conversion $d = e^{3.469}$.

scoring_points.description: Correct prediction — $T(50) = 12 + 5.189\ln(50) = 12 + 5.189 \cdot 3.912 = 12 + 20.299 \approx 32.299$ degrees Celsius (accept values in $[32.2, 32.4]$); supporting work must show substitution

required_elements: Correct prediction — $T(50) = 12 + 5.189\ln(50) = 12 + 5.189 \cdot 3.912 = 12 + 20.299 \approx 32.299$ degrees Celsius (accept values in $[32.2, 32.4]$); supporting work must show substitution
alternatives.correct_example: Correct prediction — $T(50) = 12 + 5.189\ln(50) = 12 + 5.189 \cdot 3.912 = 12 + 20.299 \approx 32.299$ degrees Celsius (accept values in $[32.2, 32.4]$); supporting work must show substitution

scoring_points.description: Correct depth — $12 + 5.189\ln(d) = 30 \Rightarrow 5.189\ln(d) = 18 \Rightarrow \ln(d) = \frac{18}{5.189} \approx 3.469 \Rightarrow d = e^{3.469} \approx 32.1$ meters (accept values in $[31.5, 32.7]$); supporting work must show isolating $\ln(d)$ and converting to exponential form

required_elements: Correct depth — $12 + 5.189\ln(d) = 30 \Rightarrow 5.189\ln(d) = 18 \Rightarrow \ln(d) = \frac{18}{5.189} \approx 3.469 \Rightarrow d = e^{3.469} \approx 32.1$ meters (accept values in $[31.5, 32.7]$); supporting work must show isolating $\ln(d)$ and converting to exponential form
alternatives.correct_example: Correct depth — $12 + 5.189\ln(d) = 30 \Rightarrow 5.189\ln(d) = 18 \Rightarrow \ln(d) = \frac{18}{5.189} \approx 3.469 \Rightarrow d = e^{3.469} \approx 32.1$ meters (accept values in $[31.5, 32.7]$); supporting work must show isolating $\ln(d)$ and converting to exponential form

parts.prompt:
(i) The geologist considers a linear model $T_L(d) = 12 + 0.68d$ fitted through $d = 1$ and $d = 25$. Find the value of $T_L(9)$ and compare it to the actual data value $T(9) = 22.6$°C. Determine whether the linear model overestimates or underestimates at $d = 9$.

(ii) Use the logarithmic model $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ to solve the inequality $T(d) > 25$. Determine the range of depths $d$ for which the model predicts the soil temperature exceeds $25^\circ$C. Express your answer as a decimal approximation with supporting work.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), the linear slope is $(28.7 - 12.0)/(25 - 1) = 16.7/24 \approx 0.696$; using $0.68$ from the prompt gives $T_L(9) \approx 18.12$°C vs. actual $22.6$°C, so the linear model underestimates. Accept any correct comparison that identifies the sign of the error. For part (ii), $13/5.189 \approx 2.506$, $e^{2.506} \approx 12.26$ m. Both the isolation of $\ln(d)$ and the conversion $d = e^{2.506}$ must appear.

scoring_points.description: Correct value and comparison — $T_L(9) = 12 + 0.68(9) = 12 + 6.12 = 18.12^\circ$C; this is less than the actual $22.6^\circ$C, so the linear model underestimates the temperature at $d = 9$

required_elements: Correct value and comparison — $T_L(9) = 12 + 0.68(9) = 12 + 6.12 = 18.12^\circ$C; this is less than the actual $22.6^\circ$C, so the linear model underestimates the temperature at $d = 9$
alternatives.correct_example: Correct value and comparison — $T_L(9) = 12 + 0.68(9) = 12 + 6.12 = 18.12^\circ$C; this is less than the actual $22.6^\circ$C, so the linear model underestimates the temperature at $d = 9$

scoring_points.description: Correct inequality solution — $12 + 5.189\ln(d) > 25 \Rightarrow 5.189\ln(d) > 13 \Rightarrow \ln(d) > \frac{13}{5.189} \approx 2.506 \Rightarrow d > e^{2.506} \approx 12.26$ meters (accept values in $[12.1, 12.4]$); supporting work must show the isolation of $\ln(d)$ and the conversion to exponential form

required_elements: Correct inequality solution — $12 + 5.189\ln(d) > 25 \Rightarrow 5.189\ln(d) > 13 \Rightarrow \ln(d) > \frac{13}{5.189} \approx 2.506 \Rightarrow d > e^{2.506} \approx 12.26$ meters (accept values in $[12.1, 12.4]$); supporting work must show the isolation of $\ln(d)$ and the conversion to exponential form
alternatives.correct_example: Correct inequality solution — $12 + 5.189\ln(d) > 25 \Rightarrow 5.189\ln(d) > 13 \Rightarrow \ln(d) > \frac{13}{5.189} \approx 2.506 \Rightarrow d > e^{2.506} \approx 12.26$ meters (accept values in $[12.1, 12.4]$); supporting work must show the isolation of $\ln(d)$ and the conversion to exponential form
